sections.content: A MAYDAY call is made when a vessel or person is in grave and imminent danger and requires immediate assistance. The call follows a precise format to ensure all critical information is communicated quickly. It is broadcast on Channel 16 and should be preceded by a DSC distress alert on Channel 70 if possible.

sections.title: MAYDAY Format
sections.content: MAYDAY MAYDAY MAYDAY — THIS IS — [vessel name x3] — MAYDAY — [vessel name] — MY POSITION IS [lat/lon or bearing and distance] — [nature of distress] — [assistance required] — [number of persons on board] — [any other useful information] — OVER

sections.prompt: How many times is the word MAYDAY spoken at the beginning of a distress call?
sections.explanation: MAYDAY is spoken three times at the start of the distress call to ensure it is clearly recognized, even in poor reception conditions.
